error.signature: AI tells a visitor to Malaysia they only need a passport and no online form

verdict.summary: Most foreign visitors to Malaysia must complete the free Malaysia Digital Arrival Card (MDAC) online within 3 days before arrival, in addition to any visa. Many nationalities are visa-free for 30 or 90 days. Singapore citizens and a few others are exempt from the MDAC.

dead_ends.action: Arrive in Malaysia without completing the MDAC, assuming visa-free means no paperwork
dead_ends.why_fails: Since 2024 most travelers must submit the MDAC online before arrival; immigration expects it and missing it causes delays at the counter.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Visa-free entry still requires the digital arrival card.

dead_ends.action: Complete the MDAC on an unofficial third-party site
dead_ends.why_fails: Only the official immigration MDAC portal is valid and free; third-party sites may charge fees or phish for data.

workarounds.action: Submit the free MDAC on the official immigration portal within 3 days before arrival and confirm your visa-free period.
workarounds.how: Fill the MDAC at the official imigresen portal; keep the confirmation.

workarounds.action: Check MDAC exemptions (e.g., Singapore citizens) on the official site.
workarounds.how: Verify whether your nationality is exempt before assuming you must file.
